statusLine: EU fuel security remains under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite average stock levels marginally above the 90-day mandate.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure across multiple countries, with 19 of 27 EU member states maintaining critical stock levels in at least one fuel category. While EU-wide averages show petrol at 98.7 days, diesel at 97.4 days, and jet fuel at 91.1 days—all narrowly above the mandatory 90-day minimum—this masks severe disparities at the national level.

Romania presents the most acute risk, with all three fuel types in critical status: petrol reserves at just 43.6 days, diesel at a concerning 20.2 days, and jet fuel at 60 days. Poland, Spain, Luxembourg, and Germany also maintain critical levels across all fuel categories, with Poland's jet fuel reserves particularly low at 21.3 days. Even major economies like France and Italy show critical petrol and jet fuel stocks, highlighting the widespread nature of current supply constraints.

The recent 9.07% drop in Brent crude prices to $90.38 per barrel provides some relief from the 27% increase since Middle East conflicts began, yet fuel prices remain elevated with diesel averaging €2.021 per liter. Northern European countries show stronger reserve positions, with Estonia, Slovenia, and Ireland maintaining safe levels across all categories, while Finland and the Netherlands demonstrate the mixed picture with strong diesel reserves but critical jet fuel stocks.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries maintain critical stock levels in at least one fuel type, with Romania showing the most severe shortages across all categories
Major economies including Germany, France, Italy, Spain, and Poland all hold critical reserves in multiple fuel types
Jet fuel emerges as the most constrained fuel, with 17 countries below safe levels despite an EU average of 91.1 days
Diesel stocks show the strongest position with an EU average of 97.4 days, supported by exceptionally high reserves in Netherlands (215.7 days) and Finland (247 days)
Brent crude prices declined 9.07% to $90.38 per barrel but remain elevated from pre-conflict levels, with fuel prices reflecting ongoing supply pressures
